-- 2 of 17 --

1	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Last year, Donna Doyle made $156,250 working just 5
hours a day from her beautiful home at the Jersey Shore.
Her home-based business is on track to hit over $200,000
in gross billings during the next few months.
You don’t need any special experience or formal training.
You don’t need expensive software or equipment… just a telephone and the
computer you already have.
You can get paid three, four, even five times for the same project – and start
making money almost immediately.
You set your own hours and live wherever you want.
And you earn a CEO’s income without ever leaving your living room!
Take Donna Doyle. For the past 7 years, she’s been living the kind of lifestyle you
may have always dreamed of.
While her neighbors start their days bleary-eyed, rushing for the 7:08 into Penn
Station or driving an hour in heavy traffic to make it to the office on time…
Donna sits blissfully at home, in front of her computer and phone. She can watch
the birds jockeying for position at the bird feeder at the far end of her backyard and
sip a cup of Earl Grey tea while reading, chatting with clients, or setting her schedule
for the coming week.
While others are stuck in dead-end jobs or mind-numbing meetings, she picks
and chooses the projects she wants to work on – as well as the people she wants to
work with.
And you may be able to make much more.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 17 --

2 	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Marketing for Small Businesses Is BIG Business
Here’s the deal: Small-business owners may be experts at delivering their particular
product or service. But 90% of them lack the ONE skill that will put them over the
top: HOW TO MARKET.
As professionals, they know that effective marketing often means the difference
between wild success and plodding mediocrity. But the vast majority of them are not
marketing specialists. And their resources are limited.
Sure, they can hire an advertising agency – if they can afford the exorbitant fees.
And most business owners simply don’t have the time, ability, or expertise to create
effective marketing materials on their own.
They don’t have a stable of copywriters and graphic designers at their disposal.
They have no idea how to market through the maze of the Internet, set up a website,
or write a basic sales letter.
Even creating a simple newspaper ad or catalog is a struggle when you don’t have
the knowledge or tools to do it right.
But while a small-business owner may balk at paying tens of thousands of dollars
for a single component of a promotional campaign, they’ll happily pay a retainer of
$1,000, $2,500, even $5,000 a month or more for a small-business marketing expert
– a “desktop marketer” who can handle 100% of their advertising needs and deliver
real results.
And here’s something else you may not know: There’s a HUGE shortage of
desktop marketers with the skills and knowledge to help small businesses compete
in the marketplace… and thanks to the popularity of the Internet, the demand has
increased tenfold.
This opens a huge door of opportunity for the right people with the right tools.
People like you!
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 17 --

3	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Small-Business Owners Are Frantically Seeking
Skilled People Who Can Produce Profit-Boosting…
Website content	•
Email blasts	•
Direct-response letters	•
Sales inserts	•
Magazine ads	•
Catalogs	•
Inserts	•
Online advertising campaigns	•
Newsletters	•
Brochures	•
Radio and TV commercials	•
Internet promotions	•
And much, much more!	•
Exactly What Is Desktop Marketing?
Desktop marketing may be the most lucrative at-home business of the decade! It
offers you unlimited earning potential plus the ability to work when and where you
want to. If you want to earn more money, you simply take on more work. No more
punching a clock. No more bosses peering over your shoulder.
If you’re straight out of high school or college… if you’ve been a professional for
years and want out… if you’re a stay-at-home mom, retired, or just want to make
money in your spare time – you are qualified to be a desktop marketer!
There’s little competition and no overhead. You don’t need a degree or any special
training. You don’t have to be a talented writer, a gifted artist, or an experienced
marketer. You don’t need to learn any special computer programs (unless you want
to). And the only start-up capital required is a desk, a phone, and the computer you’re
already using.
Best of all, you’ll be providing an important service that thousands of small-
business owners in your area need and want right now.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 17 --

4 	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
When it comes to starting a new business, what could be better?
Hundreds of people from all walks of life and all levels of education have successfully
reinvented themselves as desktop marketing pros. And you can too – in weeks, not
months!
You’ll Have Steady, Repeat Business
Unlike a one-shot copy or design assignment, desktop marketing helps you build a
client base with strong repeat business, along with steadily rising income – two things
crucial to your long-term success.
As a desktop marketer, you’ll also become an integral part of a company’s success.
Clients will come to you because you can help them solve problems they can’t solve
themselves. Each client will have something different to intrigue and challenge you.
And as you help their sales and profits improve, your clients will return to you with
even bigger, more rewarding projects.
They are putting themselves in your hands because they trust you.
Your clients are happy because you save them thousands of dollars in time and
effort and provide them with an essential service. And you’re happy because you can
earn extra money every day – money that you didn’t have access to before. All without
leaving your kitchen table!
Direct Marketing: A $1.8 Trillion Industry
Direct-response marketing (also known as direct marketing or direct-response
advertising) is a $1.86 trillion a year industry. Moreover, this industry has been
growing for well over a decade, since it’s one of the best ways for marketers to launch
new products. Plus, it’s also a highly effective way to sell existing products and services.
So what exactly is direct-response marketing?
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 17 --

5	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Unlike most “image” or brand advertising that you see in newspapers and magazines,
direct response is an advertisement or letter that asks the reader to take an immediate
and specific action – whether it’s to order the product right then and there, or request
more information about it.
Direct response uses many types of media, including:
television	•
magazine and newspaper ads	•
mail	•
email blasts	•
banner ads	•
postcards	•
websites	•
pay-per-click ads	•
radio	•
statement inserts	•
card packs	•
Basically, if an ad is asking the prospect to take a specific action – call an 800
number, visit a website, return a response card, place an order, complete a survey, etc.
– it is a direct-response effort.
Companies use direct response to sell everything from mattresses to financial
newsletters… from health supplements to credit cards. Why? Because a good direct-
response promotion can bring in millions of dollars in sales for a fraction of the cost
of opening a store or selling wholesale to retailers.
Direct-response marketing allows companies to sell directly to those people
most likely to buy their products. And it can be done easily and simply – by sending
letters or emails to those who have shown an interest in buying similar products in
the past. It’s a particularly attractive option for small-business owners, because it can
communicate complete information about a product or service, as well as help them
reach their particular target audience efficiently and cost-effectively.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 17 --

6 	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Fundamentals of Design for a Desktop Marketer
Let’s take a look at the first skill you’ll need to know about in this great career –
graphic design.
Now you may not want to be a graphic designer, and that’s okay. As a freelance
marketer, all you really need to know are the basic concepts that go into graphic
design. So you’ll want to be sure to work through the program exercises. And who
knows? You may even find that you enjoy graphic design for direct response.
Consider the following:
It’s now harder to get into the Rhode Island School of Design than it is to	•
get into Harvard Business School. (Fortunately, you don’t need a degree to
make a very good living as a graphic designer.)
In the United States alone, the number of graphic designers has increased	•
tenfold in the last decade. (And the demand for good graphic designers
shows no sign of slowing.)
More and more, even “Industrial Era” corporations – like BMW and	•
General Motors – are viewing their products from a design perspective.
This growing competition for market attention only increases your value as
a graphic designer.
Reason: Without a designer’s touch, marketing messages are likely to go unread!
Graphic design isn’t so much concerned with being creative or focused on the
designer’s sense of satisfaction or need for self-expression as it is with serving your
client’s communications needs.
Graphic designers take the elements of an existing project – often words and
photographs – and assemble them into a publication that enhances those words and
images. In doing so, the designer creates a “whole” that’s greater than the sum of its
parts.
Throughout the project, the designer focuses on the client’s communication goals
rather than “How do I feel about this?”
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 17 --

7	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
In many ways, graphic designers go through the same steps you would to assemble
a jigsaw puzzle. You would…
Start with a box of individual puzzle pieces; by itself, each piece lacks	•
meaning and doesn’t communicate.
Glance at the illustration on the cover of the box to get an idea of the	•
desired end result.
Through trial and error, try placing pieces in different locations until you	•
find the one place where each piece fits.
When you’re through, the individual pieces create a whole that’s greater than their
sum – a single image that tells a story and appeals to your readers’ emotions.
The three-step process that completes a jigsaw puzzle is the same process that
graphic designers go through when they receive a new assignment from a client.
However, instead of getting direction from the “picture on the cover of the box,”
graphic designers ask their clients questions and take careful note of the answers. No
detail is too small for a graphic designer who wants to attract the best clients possible!
8 Building Blocks of a Successful Design Career
There is no single secret that guarantees a successful design career.
Instead, there are eight elements involved in the field of design. Let’s take a look at
each of these elements…
1. Your capability to market yourself
2. Hardware and software resources
3. Abilities and talents
4. Motivation
5. Asking the right questions
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 17 --

8 	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
6. Existing corporate standards
7. Project components, including:
Word processing files such as headlines, subheads, body copy, captions, and	•
quotations
Graphic images such as photographs, scanned images, printed black-and-	•
white pictures and color slides, stock photographs, illustrations, rules,
borders, fills, graduated fills, bleeds, and graphic accents
8. And finally, making appropriate decisions
Fundamentals of Copywriting for a Desktop Marketer
Copywriting is another critical element of desktop marketing. Any marketing
campaign starts with “copy” – the written words. Then, the writer sends the copy to
the graphic designer for layout and adding images. Finally, the project is printed and
mailed, or simply sent to prospects via the Internet.
Copywriting, simply put, is any writing that offers a product or service for sale.
Whether writing for a store, a consultant, a website or a nonprofit organization,
copywriting is selling. A good copywriter is a good salesperson. They persuade
readers to take action: send for information, buy now, make a donation, call for a free
consultation, or try a free sample product. It’s all about selling.
And selling by writing promotional and educational material for local and national
businesses and organizations can be extremely lucrative. Clients hire freelancers to
produce a wide range of materials – annual reports, direct-mail letters (such as lead
generating, sales, fundraising, and magalogs), brochures, space ads, postcards, catalog
copy, radio and TV commercials, newsletters (print and email), and websites, to name
a few.
As a desktop marketer, you can either hire a professional copywriter or write your
own copy. And with this program, you might discover you really enjoy writing and
decide you want to take on that part of your business… making it even more profitable
for you!
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 17 --

9	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Creating a Publication Framework
The starting point for creating a structure for your publications is to list the
expected contents. This involves analyzing the specific text and graphic elements your
publication must accommodate. You should know both the quality and quantity of
the elements you’ll be working with.
There will be plenty of time later for you to fine-tune the details of your page
layouts. All that’s necessary as you start working is to identify – and rough out –
possible layouts that satisfy your publication goals.
Working With Type
Both copy and graphics have to be visually appealing and comfortable for your
audience, or else the message is lost. It doesn’t matter how much creativity or thought
you put into your project, if it doesn’t attract and keep their attention, it won’t be
successful.
The starting point for understanding type is to recognize that it plays two major
roles in your designs. Consequently, the text formatting decisions you make must be
based on knowing which of the two categories of type you’re working with:
Type for Reading. The majority of type in your publications is intended to be
read. This type should be almost “transparent,” so your readers can concentrate on the
meaning of the words rather than their appearance.
Type for Image Recognition. Book titles, product names and packaging, and
logos (stylized treatments of a firm’s name) are meant to be “recognized” more than
“read.” For instance, most individuals instantly recognize the Coca-Cola or Pepsi-
Cola text rather than read it, word for word, for meaning.
This separation of type into two categories by intended function has two benefits.
First, it provides a logical framework for examining typeface choices; and second, it
shows how to manipulate type as you design your pages.
Your goal is to position yourself, over time, as the “obvious” provider of creative
desktop marketing services… a provider who is competent, friendly, knowledgeable,
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 17 --

10 	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
and – most importantly – the first desktop marketer who comes to mind when
marketing is mentioned.
Getting Experience
As soon as you have a graphics program set up on your computer, you can start
practicing. For starters, simply duplicate the things you see every day – advertisements
that come in the mail, business cards, movie and CD covers, flyers, menus… basically
anything and everything you can find.
Once you’re practiced, you can run an ad and start on the local level. This gives
you a chance to get some experience and success under your belt. Approach local
businesses to see if they need anything. Join your local Chamber of Commerce and
network with the business owners you meet there.
Remember, success involves various factors – all of which you can master – and
mastery of desktop marketing can be the start of a very lucrative career.
Resources
Books
The Business Side of Creativity: The Complete Guide for Running a Graphic
Design or Communications Business.
Foote, Cameron S.
2002, W.W. Norton.
In-depth survey of marketing techniques.
Handbook: Pricing & Ethical Guidelines.
Graphic Artists Guild.
2003, Graphic Artists Guild Handbook.
Strengths include fee variations in different parts of the country.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

11	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Now that you’ve had an introduction to this field, you can be off and running.
However, if you want the short cut to big success in this exploding industry,
you can sign up for our one-of-a-kind program: Desktop Marketing for Success,
Independence and Income.
Desktop marketing is one of the most interesting, rewarding, and profitable
businesses there are. No two projects are alike… and each one is creative and
challenging. No matter where you live, there are always small-business owners looking
for desktop marketers to help them advertise their products and boost sales, especially
through the Internet.
One of the best things about being a desktop marketer is this: You can work from
your home as much – or as little – as you like. (Remember, Donna Doyle works only
4 or 5 hours a day.)
A Whole New Mind: Moving From the Information Age to the Conceptual Age.
Pink, Daniel.
2005, Riverhead Books (Penguin).
Inspiration for those taught that “computer science is your best bet.”

12 	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
Here’s another major benefit: As long as you have access to a computer and an
Internet connection, “home” can be anywhere you choose.
You can live about an hour outside New York City in a charming beach community
with good schools and low crime. Or if you’re like a few other desktop marketers I know,
“home” could become a ski chalet in Telluride, a flat in London, or a terrific oceanfront
townhouse in Boca Raton.
You can find clients all over the country. But even if you want to stick with clients
from your own hometown, you could easily have a part-time or full-time business that
earns you anywhere from $30,000 to a lofty $150,000+.
Whether your client needs a space ad, a press release, even a website… Desktop
Marketing for Success, Independence and Income is your “bible” for guidance,
inspiration, and ideas. It delivers the profit-boosting business techniques that would
take years of trial-and-error to learn on your own. And it helps you avoid the costly
mistakes that can sink you – unless you had some expert guidance leading the way.
Believe me, clients will be grateful for your expertise. And willing to pay you big
bucks for it, too.
But don’t take just my word for it. Let me share a few other success stories with you.
Live the Dream Life That These People Do…
Take Lori Haller. A talented graphic designer in her own right, Lori’s business
really took off when she became a desktop marketer. And today, she makes a six-figure
income while raising three children at home. She told us:
“One of my clients couldn’t decide whether to hire an advertising agency
or hire me. Once they realized I could provide the same writing, design,
and marketing services as the agency could – and at a fraction of the cost –
I got the account.
“I routinely hire copywriters, designers, printers, and illustrators to work
with me on a variety of projects. Meanwhile, my income has risen by more
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

13	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
than 35%… and I still have plenty of time to spend with my husband and
kids. I wouldn’t give up this lifestyle for anything!”
Then there’s Dennis Rome. He ran a successful computer consulting business in
Louisiana for over 15 years. But as the 1990s wore down and the market began to
change, Dennis wanted something more. He wasn’t a writer, and the only design he
ever did was a family newsletter every Christmas.
Then, he learned about Desktop Marketing:
“I knew this was the perfect opportunity for me. Since I was a small-
business owner myself, I knew what people like me wanted and needed.”
Dennis started part-time, honing his skills. Today, just two years later, 100% of his
income is from his desktop marketing business…
“It pays a lot better – and it’s far more fun than dealing with computer
woes!”
Jennifer Osterhouse decided to become a desktop marketer after dealing with one-
too-many rush hours on the Washington, DC beltway:
“No matter what time I left work, the Beltway was like a parking lot. I
was exhausted from the day, but still had to pick my kids up from day care
before six. I was so stressed out all the time. I was too tired to enjoy any
family time.”
Since Jennifer was a graphic designer, she started with a few projects from her
former employer…
“Then, as my client base grew, I hired copywriters, illustrators,
webmasters, and even other graphic designers so I could get the job done.
“My clients now look to me as a resource for marketing information, print
buying, even media placement. And because I have such a wide stable of
talented people at my disposal, I never have to turn anything away.”
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

14 	Become a Desktop Marketing Whiz
At the beginning of 2003, Mike Klassen was testing software and writing technical
manuals for Microsoft. As Mike could tell you, working at Microsoft was like being in
a pressure-cooker:
“I was working for one of the greatest and most secure companies on the
planet, and I was busy looking for the exit.”
That’s when he decided to try his hand at running his own desktop marketing
business, so he started writing a few press releases and marketing communications for
local companies. But Mike quickly realized that if he could provide other services,
like graphic design, marketing advice, and production, his income – and client base
– would skyrocket. Today, Mike chooses to do most of the writing and design work
himself… and makes huge fees in the process:
“When you develop a reputation for being more than just a designer, or
just a writer, that can mean the difference between you getting the project
or someone else.”
The good news is that the Desktop Marketing for Success, Independence and
Income costs only $190. For that, you get everything you need to start and run your
own desktop marketing business.
Even better…
Just $19 Can Get You Started…
Don’t want to pay upfront? With our easy payment plan, you can get started in
your new home business for only $19.
You’ll get immediate access to the first chapter to get you started, along with two
bonus books. Then every two weeks, you’ll gain access to additional chapters for only
$19 each until you complete the program.

And it’s available to you for a start-up cost of less than 50 cents a day over your first
